exercises.name: Chest Fly Machine
exercises.notes: Keep your back flat against the pad, maintain a slight bend in your elbows, and squeeze your chest at the center. Avoid letting the weight pull your arms too far back past your shoulders.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.notes: Keep your feet flat, retract your shoulder blades, and press straight out. Do not lock your elbows at the peak to keep constant tension on the pecs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Chest Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench to 30-45 degrees. Keep your chest up, lower dumbbells to the upper chest, and press up in an arc, stopping just before your elbows lock.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Keep your triceps firmly planted on the pad. Curl the weight up, squeezing the biceps, and lower it slowly until your arms are nearly fully extended. Do not lean back.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Bicep Curls
exercises.notes: Lie back on an incline bench, letting your arms hang straight down. Curl the weights up while keeping your upper arms completely stationary to isolate the biceps.

exercises.name: Post-Workout Cardio
exercises.notes: Maintain a steady, moderate pace. Focus on deep breathing and keeping your heart rate in your target zone for cardiovascular endurance.

exercises.name: Wide Lat Pull Down
exercises.notes: Grip wide, lean back slightly, and pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back. Control the weight as it goes back up.

exercises.name: Back Row Machine
exercises.notes: Sit with your chest firmly against the pad. Pull the handles toward your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together hard at the peak contraction.

exercises.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Cross the cables and pull them outward and slightly back. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and focus entirely on contracting the back of your shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
exercises.notes: Keep your elbows pinned tightly to your sides. Push the cable down until your arms are fully extended, squeeze, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.notes: Keep your elbows close to your head and pointing up. Lower the weight behind your head until you feel a deep stretch, then extend your arms fully.

exercises.name: Post-Workout Cardio
exercises.notes: Rowing machine or incline walk. Keep your posture upright and core braced.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.notes: Place feet shoulder-width apart. Lower yourself until thighs are parallel to the platform, keeping your lower back pressed against the pad. Drive forcefully through your heels.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.notes: Keep your chest up and core engaged. Lower your back knee straight down toward the floor, ensuring your front knee tracks in line with your toes.

exercises.name: Leg Extensions
exercises.notes: Adjust the pad to sit just above your ankles. Extend your legs fully, squeeze your quads hard at the top, and lower the weight under strict control.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Keep your hips pressed firmly into the bench. Curl the pad toward your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings, and lower the weight slowly without letting the stack drop.

exercises.name: Calf Press
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the platform. Drop your heels for a deep stretch, then press up as high as possible through your big toes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit with back support. Start with dumbbells at shoulder height. Press up and slightly inward, avoiding clanking the weights together at the top.

exercises.name: Unilateral Cable Lateral Raises
exercises.notes: Stand sideways to the cable machine. Keep a slight bend in your working arm and lift the cable out to the side until your hand reaches shoulder level. Lower slowly.

exercises.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Focus on pulling with the rear delts rather than your back. Keep your chest up and avoid using momentum to swing the weight back.

exercises.name: Post-Workout Cardio
exercises.notes: Stationary bike to flush the legs out. Keep resistance low and cadence steady.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raises
exercises.notes: Hang with an overhand grip. Engage your core to curl your pelvis upward, lifting your legs until they are parallel to the floor. Lower them slowly to absolutely prevent swinging.

exercises.name: Cable Crunches
exercises.notes: Kneel below a high pulley holding the rope behind your neck. Crunch your torso downward, bringing elbows toward your knees while keeping your hips entirely stationary.

exercises.name: Russian Twists
exercises.notes: Sit leaning back at a 45-degree angle. Keep your core tight and rotate your shoulders fully from side to side, controlling the movement rather than rushing.

exercises.name: Steady State Cardio
exercises.notes: Incline walk or stationary bike. Maintain a consistent pace where you can comfortably hold a conversation to build cardiovascular endurance without taxing your nervous system.

exercises.name: Wide Lat Pull Down
exercises.notes: Maintain a tall chest. Initiate the pull by depressing your scapula, then drive the elbows down.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend. Keep your back completely flat and pull the barbell to your lower ribcage, squeezing your lats hard at the top.

exercises.name: Cable Bicep Curls
exercises.notes: Stand straight with a slight bend in your knees. Keep your elbows tucked to your sides and curl the bar up, focusing on a hard squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Bicep Curls
exercises.notes: Ensure you get a full stretch at the bottom of the movement. Keep the wrists neutral and curl smoothly.

exercises.name: Front Dumbbell Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with core braced. Lift the dumbbells straight out in front of you with a slight bend in your elbows. Stop at eye level and slowly lower.

exercises.name: Post-Workout Cardio
exercises.notes: Treadmill or stair stepper. Focus on establishing a sustainable breathing rhythm.

focus: Legs, Side Delt & Rear Delt & Chest

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.notes: Ensure your knees travel in the same direction as your toes. Do not lock your knees aggressively at the top of the movement to protect the joint.

exercises.name: Leg Extensions
exercises.notes: Hold the top position for a split second to maximize quad engagement before starting the descent.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Resist the weight on the way down; the eccentric portion is just as important as the curl itself.

exercises.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Use a light enough weight that you do not need to use momentum from your torso.

exercises.name: Chest Fly Machine
exercises.notes: Keep your shoulders retracted. Imagine hugging a large tree to maintain the slight elbow bend and focus the tension entirely on the chest fibers.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flat Chest Press
exercises.notes: Plant your feet firmly on the ground. Lower the dumbbells until they are level with your chest, keeping your elbows tucked at a 45-degree angle to your body.

exercises.name: Post-Workout Cardio
exercises.notes: Any cardio machine of choice. Keep it consistent to help flush metabolic waste from the muscles.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.notes: Rest on your forearms and toes. Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels, bracing your core as if you are about to be punched in the stomach.

exercises.name: Sleep
exercises.notes: Sleep is the most important part of recovery. Make sure you get at least 8 hours of sleep per night.
